scenarios.title: Engineering work requires the captured mission
scenarios.given: an MDE-enabled project with a captured mission
scenarios.then: the session starts from MISSION.md, project-local MDE state, and applicable central context before changing code

scenarios.title: Cranfield search uses OpenSearch-backed retrieval
scenarios.given: a user calls the Cranfield search endpoint with a non-empty query
scenarios.then: the Worker sends a generated OpenSearch multi_match request to the configured Cranfield index and maps returned hits without hardcoded query-specific results

scenarios.title: Search explain reveals the active retrieval path
scenarios.given: a user calls the Cranfield explain endpoint
scenarios.then: the response includes the incoming query, normalized query, dataset, architecture version, generated OpenSearch query, retrieval strategy, ranking logic, accepted architecture decisions, applied techniques, top result explanations, and latency metadata

scenarios.title: Live search has no runtime LLM dependency
scenarios.given: the live search source is inspected
scenarios.then: no OpenAI, Anthropic, chat completion, response generation, or Gemini runtime call is present in the search path

scenarios.title: Baseline evaluator computes relevance metrics
scenarios.then: it reports MAP, nDCG, Precision, Recall, and MRR-style metrics in a versioned experiment artifact

scenarios.title: Public Phase 1 endpoint availability
scenarios.given: the Worker and OpenSearch implementation exists
scenarios.then: a Cloudflare Worker deployment and live OpenSearch Cranfield index must be configured and validated before public readiness is claimed
scenarios.evidence: GEN-004 deployed https://retail-search.feroshjacob.workers.dev and verified public health, search, explain, latest, and versioned endpoints against cranfield-v0.

scenarios.title: Public users can try searches with indexed-data context
scenarios.given: a public user opens the deployed Retail Search URL
scenarios.when: they need to try the Cranfield baseline
scenarios.then: the first screen provides a search form, example queries, indexed document counts, searchable field weights, topic hints, and live search/explain responses backed by cranfield-v0
scenarios.evidence: GEN-005 deployed the root search interface, added metadata endpoints, and verified root UI, metadata, search, and explain behavior twice against the public Worker.

scenarios.title: Search explain shows the retrieval flow
scenarios.given: a public user reviews Search Explain for a Cranfield query
scenarios.when: the explain endpoint and UI respond
scenarios.then: they expose a step-by-step flow from query input through normalization, OpenSearch query construction, retrieval, BM25 ranking, and explanation output
scenarios.evidence: GEN-006 added retrievalFlow to the explain API, rendered it as a flow diagram in the public UI, and verified both public root and explain endpoints twice.

scenarios.title: Public users can inspect evaluation outcomes
scenarios.given: a public user reviews the Phase 1 Cranfield baseline
scenarios.when: they need to understand evaluation quality
scenarios.then: the interface and API expose aggregate metrics plus representative correct, wrong or weak, and missed-relevant examples from the live evaluation
scenarios.evidence: GEN-007 added public evaluation metrics, strong/mixed/weak examples, and /api/cranfield/evaluation plus /api/evaluation endpoints; public checks passed twice.

scenarios.title: Public pages stay focused by phase and subsection
scenarios.given: a public user opens Retail Search as more phases are planned
scenarios.then: the home page lists project phases while Phase 1 details are split into focused overview, search, data, explain, and evaluation pages so one page does not carry every artifact
scenarios.evidence: GEN-008 deployed the phase overview home page and focused Phase 1 subpages, then verified home, /phases/cranfield/* routes, /phase-1/* aliases, planned phase pages, and APIs against the public Worker.

scenarios.title: Public users can identify dataset sources
scenarios.given: a public user sees phase names such as Cranfield, BEIR, or ESCI
scenarios.when: they browse the deployed home page, Phase 1 data page, or planned phase pages
scenarios.then: the interface links to the official dataset source or clearly states that a future dataset source has not been selected yet
scenarios.evidence: GEN-009 added source references for Glasgow Cranfield, BEIR, and Amazon ESCI, marked behavior data as not selected, exposed Cranfield source URLs in /api/cranfield/meta, and verified the deployed pages and metadata endpoint.

scenarios.title: Artifacts remain permanently traceable
scenarios.given: a reader starts from a mission, article, search endpoint, experiment, architecture version, decision, benchmark report, or tag
scenarios.when: they inspect the project artifacts or local API response contracts
scenarios.then: the artifact exposes stable identifiers linking Mission, Article, Search Evolution, Architecture Version, Architecture Decision Ledger, Git Tag, search endpoint, explain endpoint, and benchmark report
scenarios.evidence: GEN-010 added M-0001, A-0002, SE-0001, ARCH-0.1, ADL-0001, v0.1.0 traceability docs, .mde/traceability.json, local /api/v0.1 aliases, traceability payloads, and scripts/validate-traceability.mjs.

scenarios.title: Evaluation failures are grouped by retrieval behavior before architecture changes
scenarios.given: live Cranfield evaluation results and qrels
scenarios.then: the evaluator reports behavior groups, query-level relevant and retrieved IDs, first relevant rank, missing relevant IDs, aggregate metric deltas, and query-level improved/worsened counts before the architecture is accepted or rejected
scenarios.evidence: GEN-011 added failure behavior analysis, detailed live baseline/query-rescue/field-sum artifacts, comparison output, SE-0002, ADL-0002, and tests for behavior grouping plus candidate query construction.

scenarios.title: Candidate reranking is opt-in and evaluated before promotion
scenarios.given: live Cranfield failure groups show zero-relevant-at-10 and late-first-relevant behavior
scenarios.then: the public default remains ARCH-0.1, the candidate is selected explicitly by architecture parameter, rerank metadata is returned, live metrics are compared against baseline and prior candidates, and transferability remains deferred until Phase 2
scenarios.evidence: GEN-012 added coverage-rerank, tested it as opt-in, evaluated it live over 225 Cranfield queries, and recorded nDCG@10 0.3095 in evaluation-live-coverage-rerank-gen012.json plus evaluation-comparison-gen012.json.

scenarios.title: Supervised LTR requires query-held-out evidence before promotion
scenarios.given: Cranfield qrels are used to train a ranking model
scenarios.when: an LTR candidate is evaluated for architecture promotion
scenarios.then: the promotion decision uses query-grouped cross-validation or transferability evidence, and in-sample gains are recorded only as capacity evidence
scenarios.evidence: GEN-020 added offline LTR evaluation with query-grouped 5-fold cross-validation; GEN-021 added boosted-tree LTR; GEN-022 added BGE features that beat refined PRF under cross-validation. GEN-023 remotely validated the simpler BGE hybrid comparator. In-sample gains remain capacity evidence only, and ARCH-0.4-candidate stays unreleased until transferability and complexity comparison.

scenarios.title: Vector hybrid candidates require remote validation before promotion
scenarios.given: an offline vector/hybrid candidate beats the current lexical candidate
scenarios.when: it is considered for architecture promotion
scenarios.then: it is first validated in a separate remote OpenSearch vector index, the public/default index remains unchanged, and promotion waits for transferability plus runtime evidence
scenarios.evidence: GEN-023 created cranfield-v0-bge-base-en-v15-gen023, validated remote BGE hybrid nDCG@10 0.3533 and binary nDCG@20 0.4926, and kept the public/default ARCH-0.1 path unchanged.

scenarios.title: Milestone endpoints expose architecture evolution honestly
scenarios.given: a reader wants to compare key Cranfield search milestones through stable URLs
scenarios.when: they call milestone search or explain endpoints
scenarios.then: ARCH-0.1 and ARCH-0.2 PRF routes force their milestone architecture, ARCH-0.3 BGE routes return an explicit runtime-not-enabled response, and the public/default endpoints remain unchanged
scenarios.evidence: GEN-024 added /api/milestones/arch-0.1/*, /api/milestones/arch-0.2-prf/*, and /api/milestones/arch-0.3-bge/* plus Worker route tests and docs/endpoints/milestone-endpoints.md.
